We need to cleanup the src\common\dsstats.shared\Units\UnitMap.cs used in src\common\dsstats.weblib\Replays\BuildChart.razor and src\common\dsstats.weblib\Replays\ReplayBuildComponent.razor
Maybe we can hamonize the src\common\dsstats.weblib\Replays\ReplayComponent.razor so we don't have to collect the unit info twice for the chart and the list.
We should create a new UnitMapNg with the aim to replace the current UnitMap with a complete static list of units with the size and color info.
We should use the following set of unit colors (maybe there is a better way than the virtual UnitColor enum ..)
    public static string GetUnitColor(UnitColor unitColor)
    {
        return unitColor switch
        {
            UnitColor.Color1 => "#C0392B",
            UnitColor.Color2 => "#9B59B6",
            UnitColor.Color3 => "#2980B9",
            UnitColor.Color4 => "#1ABC9C",
            UnitColor.Color5 => "#F1C40F",
            UnitColor.Color6 => "#D35400",
            UnitColor.Color7 => "#ECF0F1",
            UnitColor.Color8 => "#7F8C8D",
            UnitColor.Color9 => "#943126",
            UnitColor.Color10 => "#5B2C6F",
            UnitColor.Color11 => "#21618C",
            UnitColor.Color12 => "#0E6655",
            UnitColor.Color13 => "#9C640C",
            UnitColor.Color14 => "#797D7F",
            UnitColor.Color15 => "#515A5A",
            _ => "#F9EBEA"
        };
    }
We should switch to the 'old' unit sizes and use them accordingly (https://github.com/ipax77/dsstats/blob/dotnet8/src/dsstats.razorlib/Replays/ReplayPlayerChartMap.razor):
    private static (double, string) GetColorAndRadius(MapUnit mapUnit, SpawnInfo spawnInfo)
    {
        if (spawnInfo.BuildUnits.TryGetValue(mapUnit.Name, out var buildUnit)
            && buildUnit is not null)
        {
            var radius = buildUnit.Size switch
            {
                UnitSize.VerySmall => 3,
                UnitSize.Small => 6,
                UnitSize.Normal => 10,
                UnitSize.Big => 15,
                UnitSize.Hero => 20,
                UnitSize.VeryBig => 25,
                _ => 12
            };

            if (buildUnit.MovementType.HasFlag(WeaponTarget.Air))
            {
                radius += 2;
            }

            var color = HelperService.GetUnitColor(buildUnit.Color);

            return (radius, color);
        }
        else
        {
            return (12, "#EC7063");
        }
    }


Here is a complete list of unit names stored in the db for each commander (first number is the commander enum from src\common\dsstats.shared\Enums.cs):
